hero.subtitle: Connecting San Juan residents with employment, training, entrepreneurship, childcare, and family support to build a better future.
hero.cta: Discover how we can help you

home.whatIsText: OASIS 360 is a comprehensive initiative based in Villa Prades, San Juan, that connects residents of ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 with employment, training, entrepreneurship, childcare, and family support services. Funded by the U.S. Economic Development Administration (EDA), the initiative brings together multiple organizations to create real pathways to economic mobility.
home.hubText: Our community center in Villa Prades brings all services together under one roof.
home.ctaSubtitle: Get in touch and we'll connect you with the right program for you.

mejorEmpleo.name: Better Jobs for Me
mejorEmpleo.tagline: Connecting residents with quality jobs through personalized training and mentorship.
mejorEmpleo.challenge: Average annual income in ZIP 00923/00924 is $19,318 — 20% below PR average. Only 58% of adults have a high school diploma or GED, and just 9% have a college education.
mejorEmpleo.audience: Residents of ZIP codes 00923 and 00924 seeking quality employment or better job opportunities.

emprendimiento.name: E3 Entrepreneurship
emprendimiento.tagline: Supporting entrepreneurs from idea to business growth.
emprendimiento.challenge: 25% of financial activity in the area is informal. 88% of PR business owners report difficulties obtaining permits. Only 38% of men and 20% of women are considered financially literate.
emprendimiento.step1: StartUp — Business idea development, registration, initial permits
emprendimiento.step2: Support — Technical assistance, financial literacy, access to capital
emprendimiento.step4: Alumni — Ongoing mentorship network and advanced opportunities
emprendimiento.audience: Entrepreneurs and aspiring business owners in ZIP codes 00923/00924.

comunidadDeCuidado.name: Caring Community
comunidadDeCuidado.tagline: Removing employment barriers by providing access to childcare, elder care, and after-school programs.
comunidadDeCuidado.challenge: 71% of residents with children report lack of childcare as a significant employment barrier. High childcare costs prevent 29% of the population from transitioning to full-time jobs.
services: New care business creation in the community
comunidadDeCuidado.audience: Families needing access to childcare, elder care, or after-school programs in order to work.

navegadorFamiliar.name: Family Navigator
navegadorFamiliar.tagline: Your family at the center. Dedicated navigators helping you overcome barriers and achieve stability.
navegadorFamiliar.challenge: Over 50% of families in ZIP 00923/00924 live below the poverty line. Nearly 30% of households are led by single mothers. Deep mistrust toward institutional systems due to decades of fragmented services.
navegadorFamiliar.step3: Family action plan — Co-created plans with specific goals
navegadorFamiliar.step4: Case management and referrals — Continuous support and barrier removal
services: Referrals to employment, training, childcare, health, and housing services
Individualized case management or on-demand navigation
navegadorFamiliar.audience: Families in ZIP codes 00923/00924 needing comprehensive support to overcome barriers to stability.

empoderados.name: Empowered for Employment (E2)
empoderados.tagline: Changing the rules of the game so that working doesn't mean losing your benefits.
empoderados.partner: IDJ (Instituto del Desarrollo de la Juventud)
empoderados.challenge: 60% of non-working families cite fear of losing government benefits as their primary barrier to employment. Families earning above ~$29,000 lose government assistance eligibility, creating employment disincentives — the "benefit cliff."
empoderados.step2: Community advisory council — Residents designing policy from lived experience
empoderados.step3: Pilot programs — "El Poder de Saber" and Family Development Accounts
empoderados.step4: Narrative change — Campaign on benefit transitions
services: Understanding of how employment affects your benefits
empoderados.audience: Families fearing benefit loss when entering employment and those seeking to change the policies creating these barriers.

hub.title: The OASIS Hub
hub.subtitle: Our community center in Villa Prades, San Juan
hub.description: The OASIS Hub is the heart of our initiative — a physical space where all services and programs converge under one roof, facilitating access to opportunities for the entire community.
hub.educationDesc: Vimenti Charter School offers quality education from kindergarten through 12th grade.
hub.workforceDesc: Over 300 individuals trained annually in market-demanded skills.
hub.healthcareDesc: Vidalús Community Health Clinic serves over 4,000 patients annually.
hub.entrepreneurshipDesc: Project Makers supports 100+ entrepreneurs with incubation and acceleration.
hub.youthDesc: Over 550 children and youth participate in after-school programs.
hub.address: Villa Prades, San Juan, Puerto Rico

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.subtitle: Organizations committed to transforming our community
partners.vimentiDesc: Family Navigator, Better Jobs for Me, Charter School K-12. Vimenti leads comprehensive employment and family support programs.
partners.projectMakersDesc: E3 Entrepreneurship incubator and accelerator. Project Makers supports entrepreneurs from idea to business growth.
partners.idjDesc: Research, public policy, and benefit cliff programs. IDJ works to change the rules preventing economic progress.
partners.goodwillDesc: Adult education and workforce development referrals. Goodwill's Excel Center offers diploma and training opportunities.
partners.psiDesc: Overall coordination, governance, and Caring Community program. PSI leads the OASIS 360 initiative and its operations.
partners.ctaText: If your organization is interested in collaborating with OASIS 360, we'd love to talk.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: We're here to help you find the right program for you and your family.

footer.attribution: This initiative is funded by the U.S. Economic Development Administration (EDA) through the RECOMPETE Plan — Grant ED24HDQ0G0516.
